title: betula microphylla var. occidentalis
intro: Betula microphylla var. occidentalis, also known as the Western dwarf birch, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species native to the mountainous regions of Western North America. The bark, leaves, and twigs of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Betula microphylla var. occidentalis include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating wounds, as well as acting as a natural antiseptic to prevent infections. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
